terms.definition: The value of a digit based on its position, from ones through millions.
terms.example: In 3,456,789 the 4 is in the hundred thousands place (400,000).
terms.kid_friendly: Where a digit sits decides how big it is.

terms.definition: Numbers like 10, 100, 1000 written as 10 raised to an exponent.
terms.kid_friendly: 10 multiplied by itself a certain number of times.

terms.definition: A small number written above and to the right that tells how many times to use the base as a factor.
terms.kid_friendly: The tiny number that says how many times to multiply.

terms.definition: The number that is multiplied by itself.
terms.kid_friendly: The big number under the exponent.

terms.definition: Writing a number as a sum of each digit times a power of 10.
terms.kid_friendly: Break the number into digit x power-of-ten pieces.

terms.definition: Rules for the sequence: parentheses, exponents, multiply/divide left to right, add/subtract left to right.
terms.kid_friendly: PEMDAS -- the order you do math steps.

terms.definition: A math phrase with numbers and operations but no equal sign.
terms.kid_friendly: A math sentence without an answer yet.

terms.definition: Parentheses, brackets, or braces that show which operations to do first.
terms.kid_friendly: Symbols that say do me first!

terms.definition: The result of multiplication.
terms.kid_friendly: The answer when you multiply.

terms.definition: The result of division.
terms.kid_friendly: The answer when you divide.

terms.definition: The amount left over when a number does not divide evenly.
terms.kid_friendly: The leftover after making equal groups.

terms.definition: The number being divided.
terms.kid_friendly: The total you are splitting up.

terms.definition: The number you divide by.
terms.kid_friendly: The number that tells the group size.

terms.definition: A division strategy that subtracts multiples of the divisor step by step.
terms.kid_friendly: Divide in friendly chunks, then add the chunks.

terms.definition: A number with digits to the right of a decimal point.
terms.kid_friendly: A dot that shows parts smaller than 1.

terms.definition: First place right of the decimal point; 1/10.
terms.kid_friendly: One of ten equal slices.

terms.definition: Second place right of the decimal point; 1/100.
terms.kid_friendly: One of a hundred equal slices.

terms.definition: Third place right of the decimal point; 1/1000.
terms.kid_friendly: One of a thousand tiny equal slices.

terms.definition: Each place to the right of the decimal is 1/10 of the place to its left.
terms.kid_friendly: Moving right makes things 10 times smaller.

terms.definition: Replace a decimal with the nearest value at a given place.
terms.kid_friendly: Find the closest shorter decimal.

terms.definition: A number written as a/b representing a parts out of b equal parts.
terms.kid_friendly: A piece of something.

terms.definition: Top number of a fraction; how many parts.
terms.kid_friendly: How many pieces you have.

terms.definition: Bottom number of a fraction; total equal parts.
terms.kid_friendly: How many pieces the whole was cut into.

terms.definition: Fractions that represent the same value.
terms.kid_friendly: Different names, same size.

terms.definition: A fraction whose numerator and denominator have no common factor other than 1.
terms.kid_friendly: The smallest way to write a fraction.

terms.definition: A whole number plus a fraction.
terms.kid_friendly: A whole part and a fraction part together.

terms.definition: A fraction where the numerator is >= the denominator.
terms.kid_friendly: Top number is bigger -- more than one whole.

terms.definition: Fractions with different bottom numbers.
terms.kid_friendly: The bottom numbers do not match.

terms.definition: A shared multiple of the denominators used to add or subtract fractions.
terms.kid_friendly: Make the bottoms the same so you can add.

terms.definition: The smallest common multiple of the denominators.
terms.kid_friendly: The smallest number both bottoms go into.

terms.definition: Multiply numerators, then denominators.
terms.kid_friendly: Top x top, bottom x bottom.

terms.definition: Multiply by the reciprocal of the divisor.
terms.kid_friendly: Keep-Change-Flip, then multiply.

terms.definition: Flip the numerator and denominator.
terms.kid_friendly: Flip the fraction upside down.

terms.definition: Multiply the fraction by the whole number.
terms.kid_friendly: Take a piece of a number.

terms.definition: A flat surface formed by a horizontal x and vertical y number line that cross at the origin.
terms.kid_friendly: A grid with an x-line and a y-line for plotting points.

terms.definition: The point (0, 0) where the x-axis and y-axis meet.
terms.kid_friendly: The center start point of the grid.

terms.definition: The horizontal number line on a coordinate plane.
terms.example: The x-axis runs left to right.
terms.kid_friendly: The flat line going sideways.

terms.definition: The vertical number line on a coordinate plane.
terms.example: The y-axis runs up and down.
terms.kid_friendly: The line going up and down.

terms.definition: Two numbers (x, y) that locate a point on the coordinate plane.
terms.kid_friendly: An address for a point: over then up.

terms.definition: The amount of space a 3D figure occupies, measured in cubic units.
terms.example: A box 4x3x2 has volume 24 cubic cm.
terms.kid_friendly: How much stuff fits inside a solid shape.
terms.visual_hint: V = l x w x h

terms.definition: A cube with side length 1 used to measure volume.
terms.kid_friendly: A tiny cube that measures space.

terms.definition: A 3D shape with 6 rectangular faces.
terms.example: A cereal box is a rectangular prism.
terms.kid_friendly: A box shape.

terms.definition: A cube with each edge 1 unit long; volume = 1 cubic unit.
terms.example: Fill a box with unit cubes to find its volume.
terms.kid_friendly: A tiny 1x1x1 building block.

terms.definition: Changing from one unit to another using multiplication or division.
terms.kid_friendly: Switching units, like feet to inches.

terms.definition: A graph using marks above a number line to show data frequency.
terms.kid_friendly: X marks on a number line to count data.
terms.visual_hint: X X  X X X  X

terms.definition: An ordered set of numbers or shapes following a rule.
terms.kid_friendly: Something that grows or repeats by a rule.

terms.definition: Numbers, variables, and operations without an equal sign.
terms.kid_friendly: A math phrase -- no answer shown yet.

terms.definition: A letter that stands for an unknown number.
terms.kid_friendly: A letter that hides a number.

terms.definition: A classification system where shapes are grouped by shared attributes.
terms.example: All squares are rectangles, but not all rectangles are squares.
terms.kid_friendly: A family tree of shapes.

terms.definition: A flat shape with only length and width.
terms.kid_friendly: A flat shape -- no thickness.

terms.definition: A solid shape with length, width, and height.
terms.kid_friendly: A shape you can hold -- has depth.
